text:
SURGICAL PATHOLOGY REPORT

Patient: Marlena Okonkwo
Date of Birth: [on file]
MRN: [on file]
Accession Number: SP-XXXXXX
Ordering Physician: Oncology / Breast Surgery
Clinical History: 38-year-old premenopausal woman with a self-detected left breast mass. Family history of breast and ovarian cancer (maternal lineage). No prior breast biopsies. No exogenous hormone use.

---

SPECIMEN(S) SUBMITTED

Part A: Left breast, upper outer quadrant, core needle biopsy (ultrasound-guided, 14-gauge, 5 cores)
Part B: Left axillary lymph node, fine-needle aspiration (FNA), ultrasound-guided

---

DATE OF PROCEDURE
[Procedure date on file]

---

GROSS PATHOLOGY

Part A (Left breast core biopsy): Received in formalin, labeled with patient name and "left breast mass." Consists of five tan-white to tan-pink cylindrical core fragments ranging from 1.0 to 1.6 cm in length and approximately 0.1 cm in diameter. Cores are firm, with areas appearing pale and gritty on cut surface suggestive of sclerosis/desmoplasia. All five cores are submitted entirely in two cassettes (A1-A2).

Part B (Left axillary lymph node FNA): Received as two alcohol-fixed smears and one air-dried smear, labeled "left axillary lymph node FNA," accompanied by a cell block prepared from rinsing material. The smears show adequate cellularity. Prepared smears and cell block submitted in full.

---

MICROSCOPIC FINDINGS

Part A -- Left Breast Core Needle Biopsy:

All five cores are examined. The predominant finding is a high-grade invasive carcinoma infiltrating a desmoplastic fibrous stroma. The tumor cells are arranged in solid sheets and irregular nests with focal central comedo-type necrosis. Individual cells show markedly enlarged, pleomorphic nuclei with coarse chromatin, prominent macronucleoli, and an increased nuclear-to-cytoplasmic ratio. Cytoplasm is scant to moderate and eosinophilic. Occasional multinucleated giant tumor cells are noted.

Mitotic figures are numerous and include atypical forms. By hot-spot analysis, mitotic count is in the highest category.

Nottingham Histologic Grade: Grade 3 (poorly differentiated)
  - Tubule/gland formation: Score 3 (minimal or absent tubule formation, <10%)
  - Nuclear pleomorphism: Score 3 (marked variation in size and shape, prominent nucleoli)
  - Mitotic count: Score 3 (numerous mitoses per 10 high-power fields in hot spot)
  - Total Nottingham score: 9/9

Stromal tumor-infiltrating lymphocytes (TILs): A prominent lymphocytic infiltrate is present within the tumor stroma, estimated at 40% (intratumoral and stromal, scored per international TIL working group guidelines). No plasmacytic predominance. No well-formed granulomas.

Lymphovascular invasion: Present. Several foci of tumor emboli are identified within thin-walled vascular spaces at the periphery of the invasive carcinoma.

In situ carcinoma: Focal areas of high-grade ductal carcinoma in situ (DCIS) with solid and comedo growth pattern and central necrosis are present at the periphery of the core, associated with periductal fibrosis and calcifications.

Calcifications: Present, associated with areas of necrosis and DCIS.

Background breast parenchyma: Fibrocystic changes including mild columnar cell change and scattered benign glandular elements. No atypical ductal hyperplasia identified in the sampled tissue.

Part B -- Left Axillary Lymph Node FNA:

The smears and cell block show a dual population consisting of occasional residual lymphoid cells (small lymphocytes with scant cytoplasm) and numerous large, atypical epithelial cells. The atypical cells have enlarged, irregular nuclei with prominent nucleoli and are present in loose cohesive clusters and singly. The morphology is consistent with metastatic carcinoma. The atypical cells show moderate to abundant cytoplasm. Background is proteinaceous with scattered lymphoid cells. No necrotic debris.

Impression: Metastatic carcinoma, morphology consistent with breast primary (see IHC below).

---

ANCILLARY STUDIES / IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY

IHC performed on Part A (formalin-fixed, paraffin-embedded core biopsy material):

Estrogen Receptor (ER) [SP1 clone]:
  Result: NEGATIVE
  Staining: 0% of tumor nuclei stain positive
  Intensity: 0 (no staining)
  Allred Score: 0/8
  Internal positive control (normal ductal epithelium): Satisfactory

Progesterone Receptor (PR) [1E2 clone]:
  Result: NEGATIVE
  Staining: 0% of tumor nuclei stain positive
  Intensity: 0 (no staining)
  Allred Score: 0/8
  Internal positive control: Satisfactory

HER2 [4B5 clone, Ventana]:
  Result: NEGATIVE (IHC score 1+)
  Staining: Incomplete, faint, incomplete membrane staining in a proportion of tumor cells
  Interpretation: Score 1+ (negative); FISH not performed per CAP/ASCO guidelines (IHC 1+)
  Note: No areas of 3+ staining or complete circumferential intense membrane staining identified

Ki-67 [MIB-1 clone]:
  Result: 85% of tumor cell nuclei demonstrate positive nuclear staining
  Interpretation: High proliferative index

CK7: Positive (diffuse, moderate-to-strong)
CK20: Negative
E-cadherin: Positive (membranous, intact) -- supports ductal (not lobular) morphology
GATA3: Positive (strong nuclear staining in majority of tumor cells) -- supports breast primary
Mammaglobin: Focally positive
Gross Cystic Disease Fluid Protein-15 (GCDFP-15): Focally positive
P63: Negative (no myoepithelial layer identified surrounding invasive nests, confirms invasive morphology)
SMA: Negative (absent myoepithelial layer)

PD-L1 [22C3 pharmDx assay, Dako]:
  Combined Positive Score (CPS): 15
  Interpretation: POSITIVE (threshold for positivity >= 10)
  Scoring: Numerator includes PD-L1 staining in tumor cells, lymphocytes, and macrophages within tumor-associated stroma; denominator is total viable tumor cells x 100
  Note: Scoring performed by pathologist using standardized CPS methodology

IHC performed on Part B (cell block from left axillary FNA):

CK7: Positive
GATA3: Positive (nuclear)
ER: Negative
PR: Negative
HER2: 1+ (negative)
  Interpretation: Metastatic carcinoma consistent with breast primary, triple-negative phenotype

---

DIAGNOSIS

Part A -- LEFT BREAST, UPPER OUTER QUADRANT, CORE NEEDLE BIOPSY:
  - INVASIVE DUCTAL CARCINOMA (INVASIVE CARCINOMA OF NO SPECIAL TYPE), NOTTINGHAM GRADE 3 (SCORE 9/9)
  - Lymphovascular invasion: PRESENT
  - Stromal tumor-infiltrating lymphocytes (TILs): 40%
  - Focal high-grade DCIS with comedo necrosis, associated with calcifications
  - Receptor status: ER NEGATIVE / PR NEGATIVE / HER2 NEGATIVE (IHC 1+) -- TRIPLE-NEGATIVE
  - Ki-67: 85% (HIGH)
  - PD-L1 CPS: 15 (POSITIVE, 22C3 assay)

Part B -- LEFT AXILLARY LYMPH NODE, FINE-NEEDLE ASPIRATION:
  - POSITIVE FOR MALIGNANT CELLS
  - METASTATIC CARCINOMA, CONSISTENT WITH BREAST PRIMARY
  - IHC profile (on cell block): CK7+, GATA3+, ER-, PR-, HER2 1+

---

COMMENT

The morphologic and immunophenotypic findings are consistent with a poorly differentiated invasive ductal carcinoma (invasive carcinoma of no special type) of the left breast, upper outer quadrant. The triple-negative receptor phenotype (ER-/PR-/HER2-), high Ki-67 proliferative index, and elevated stromal TILs at 40% are in keeping with the immunomodulatory subtype of TNBC. The elevated PD-L1 CPS of 15 by the 22C3 assay meets the threshold for PD-L1 positivity in breast cancer. Lymphovascular invasion is present. Left axillary lymph node FNA is positive for metastatic carcinoma.

These findings have been discussed with the referring clinical team. Pathologic staging contribution: pT2 (3.1 cm by imaging correlate), cN1 (biopsy-confirmed axillary node). Complete pathologic staging deferred to resection specimen.

Prepared and reviewed by:
[Attending Pathologist, MD]
Department of Surgical Pathology
[Institution]

[Electronic signature and timestamp on file]
